name: Semi-realistic pokemon RPG
description:
Welcome to the Realized World of Pokémon: Where Legends Cast Long Shadows

This is not the journey you remember.

The Kanto-Johto War ended twenty-three years ago. Twelve thousand died in four days of ice and fire over Indigo Plateau. The United Regions Alliance rose from the ashes to ensure it never happens again—and every region that signed the Akala Accord gave up something to make that promise real. The peace has held. The vigilance never ends.

Regions carry their histories like scars. Kanto rebuilt with steel and surveillance, its cities fortified, its wilds still glowing faintly in places where the toxins never faded. Johto mourns openly—the Brass Tower is a crater, the Bell Tower rings only at funerals, and the resistance is unofficial, disavowed, and utterly committed. Hoenn dances on a volcano, its Draconid Clan maintaining the ancient pact that keeps Kyogre and Groudon asleep. Sinnoh stands beneath the shadow of Mount Coronet, where creation began and where Cynthia faced gods and walked away. Unova confronted its own darkness and chose to build archives instead of monuments. Kalos hides its ancient wounds beneath elegance, beauty a blade sheathed in velvet. Alola opened its shores to the world and discovered the world came with Ultra Beasts. Galar shines brightest of all—and trusts no one with the secrets that keep the lights on.

Here, a Poké Ball is a commitment, not a convenience. Gyms test judgment alongside strength—Brock fails trainers who can't navigate a tunnel collapse, and Pryce's trials ask what you're willing to sacrifice. Victory Road is not a metaphor. The wilderness between cities holds wild Pokémon shaped by habitat and hierarchy, and the routes are not always safe.

Choose your region. Earn your badges. The League Circuit runs ten months a year, and eight badges qualify you for the Conference—where sponsors watch, reputations are made, and the path to the Elite Four begins. Or walk a different road. Contests, specialty tournaments, the Island Challenge, the Galarian Champion Cup. The world is larger than any single arena.

Licenses

Youngster License (Age 12+): One Pokémon. Patrolled routes only. Gym battles are one-on-one, and the badge is identical to any other. Ghost, Dark, and Dragon types require therapist approval. The YPAL hotline is printed on the back of the license. "Fear the Rattata. Earn the hat."

Standard License (Age 16+): Six Pokémon. Full Gym access. Sanctioned events. Dangerous species and restricted moves require additional permits. Mandatory gear: a trauma kit, a badge case, and a working knowledge of your rights.

Elite License (Age 18+, 8 Badges Minimum): Legendary habitats. Conflict zones. Peacekeeping tours. Revoked if you prove you can't handle the responsibility—and the URA's background check is not a formality.

Factions & Agencies

United Regions Alliance (URA): Born from the ashes of the Kanto-Johto War. The URA is not a world government—it is a treaty organization with a narrow mandate: prevent apocalyptic conflicts and manage cross-regional threats. Its Legendary Monitoring Bureau tracks the beings that could end civilization. Its Disaster Response corps deploys when the sirens sound. Its authority is legal, not absolute. Every region values its protection. Few enjoy its scrutiny.

Youth Pokémon Advocacy League (YPAL): "Their Future, Their Choice." Lawyers
creator: Sante2
